title: अक्रूर स्तुति
devanagariText:
॥ श्री अक्रूर स्तुति ॥
(श्रीमद्भागवतम् १०.४०)

नमस्ते वासुदेवाय नमः संकर्षणाय च।
प्रद्युम्नायानिरुद्धाय तुभ्यं भगवते नमः॥ १॥

नमो विज्ञानमात्राय परमानन्दमूर्तये।
आत्मारामाय शान्ताय निवृत्तद्वैतदृष्टये॥ २॥

आत्मन्मायां समाविश्य सोऽहंकारं च षोडशम्।
लब्धापायोदयं लोकं पश्यन्नात्मनि चाव्ययम्॥ ३॥

त्वं ह्यस्य जन्मस्थितिसंयमान्प्रभो
गुणैरनीहोऽकृतकालशक्तिधृक्।
तत्तत्स्वभावान् प्रतिबोधयन्सतः
समीक्षयामोघविहार ईहसे॥ ४॥

त्वं मायया आत्मपरात्मचेतसां
विधूतचिन्मात्यमतिं गुणावृतम्।
सत्तामनन्तां परमस्य पुंसो
भजन्ति तत्ते करुणा विधीयताम्॥ ५॥

तस्मै नमो भगवते पुरुषोत्तमाय
यो ब्रह्मरुद्रसुरदेवमनुष्यजन्तून्।
व्यूहाय नर्तयति तानुभयत्र सूत्र-
धारो यथा स्थिरचराकृतिदारुयोषाः॥ ६॥

नमस्तुभ्यं भगवते पुरुषाय महात्मने।
वासुदेवाय कृष्णाय सात्वतां पतये नमः॥ ७॥

स्वयम्भूवे नमस्तुभ्यं शान्ताय परमात्मने।
वासुदेवाय सत्त्वाय तुभ्यं सर्वात्मने नमः॥ ८॥

नमो नमस्तेऽखिलकारणाय
निष्कारणायाद्भुतकारणाय।
सर्वागमाम्नायमहार्णवाय
नमोऽपवर्गाय परायणाय॥ ९॥

गुणारणिच्छन्नचिदुष्मपाय
तत्क्षोभविस्फूर्जितमान्यधाम्ने।
नानाबुधन्यानकवातचित्र-
जन्मार्कजूषाय नमोऽस्तु विष्णो॥ १०॥

यथार्चिषो वह्नसवः परस्पर-
भेदात्प्रतीयन्त एकात्मकस्य।
वर्णा यथार्णोऽक्षरतः परस्य
भिद्येत तत्ते वचसां विवक्षा॥ ११॥

॥ इति श्रीमद्भागवते दशमस्कन्धे अक्रूर स्तुति सम्पूर्णा ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Akrura Stuti ||
(Shrimad Bhagavatam 10.40)

Namaste Vasudevaya namah Sankarshanaya cha |
Pradyumnaya-aniruddhaya tubhyam Bhagavate namah || 1 ||

Namo vijnaana-matraya paramananda-murtaye |
Atma-aramaya shantaya nivrtta-dvaita-drishtaye || 2 ||

Atman-mayam samavishya so'hankaram cha shodashama |
Labdhapayo-dayam lokam pashyann-atmani cha-avyayam || 3 ||

Tvam hy-asya janma-sthiti-samyaman prabho
gunaih-aniho'krita-kala-shakti-dhrik |
Tat-tat-svabhavan pratibodhayan satah
samikshaya-amogha-vihara ihase || 4 ||

Tvam mayaya atma-paratma-chetasam
vidhuta-chin-matya-matim guna-avritam |
Sattam-anantam paramasya pumso
bhajanti tat-te karuna vidhiyatam || 5 ||

Tasmai namo Bhagavate Purushottamaya
yo Brahma-Rudra-sura-deva-manushya-jantun |
Vyuhaya nartayati tan-ubhayatra sutra-
dharo yatha sthira-chara-akriti-daru-yoshah || 6 ||

Namas-tubhyam Bhagavate Purushaya Mahatmane |
Vasudevaya Krishnaya Satvatam Pataye namah || 7 ||

Svayambhuve namas-tubhyam shantaya Paramatmane |
Vasudevaya sattvaya tubhyam sarva-atmane namah || 8 ||

Namo namas-te'khila-karanaya
nishkaranaya-adbhuta-karanaya |
Sarva-agama-amnaya-maha-arnavaya
namo'pavargaya para-ayanaya || 9 ||

Guna-arani-chchhanna-chid-ushma-paya
tat-kshobha-visphurjita-manya-dhamne |
Nana-budhnya-anaka-vata-chitra-
janma-arka-jushaya namo'stu Vishno || 10 ||

Yatha-archisho vahni-savah paraspara-
bhedat-pratiyanta ekatmakasya |
Varna yatha-arno'ksharatah parasya
bhidyeta tat-te vachasam vivaksha || 11 ||

|| Iti Shrimad Bhagavate Dashamaskandhe Akrura Stuti Sampurna ||
hindiMeaning:
हे वासुदेव, आपको नमस्कार! संकर्षण को नमस्कार, प्रद्युम्न और अनिरुद्ध को नमस्कार! हे भगवन्, आपको नमस्कार॥ १॥

विज्ञानस्वरूप, परमानन्दमूर्ति, आत्माराम, शान्त और द्वैतदृष्टि से रहित आपको नमस्कार॥ २॥

आपने अपनी माया में प्रवेश कर अहंकार तथा सोलह विकारों सहित लोक की सृष्टि की, किन्तु स्वयं अविनाशी रहकर आत्मा में स्थित देखते हैं॥ ३॥

हे प्रभो! आप इस जगत् की उत्पत्ति, स्थिति और संहार करते हैं, गुणों से निर्लिप्त रहकर, काल-शक्ति को धारण कर, सत्पुरुषों के स्वभावों को प्रबुद्ध करते हुए अमोघ विहार करते हैं॥ ४॥

आप माया से आत्मा-परमात्मा की चेतना वालों की बुद्धि को गुणों से आवृत करते हैं। आपकी अनन्त सत्ता को परम पुरुष भजते हैं — उन पर आपकी करुणा हो॥ ५॥

उन पुरुषोत्तम भगवान् को नमस्कार, जो ब्रह्मा, रुद्र, देवता और मनुष्यों को सूत्रधार की भाँति चर-अचर रूपी काष्ठ-पुतलियों को नचाते हैं॥ ६॥

हे भगवन्, हे पुरुष, हे महात्मन्, हे वासुदेव, हे कृष्ण, सात्वतों के पति — आपको नमस्कार॥ ७॥

स्वयम्भू, शान्त, परमात्मा, वासुदेव, सत्त्वस्वरूप, सर्वात्मा — आपको नमस्कार॥ ८॥

समस्त कारणों के कारण, अकारण, अद्भुत कारण, समस्त शास्त्रों के महासागर, मोक्षदाता, परम आश्रय — आपको बारम्बार नमस्कार॥ ९॥
benefits: Deepens devotion to Lord Krishna as Vasudeva
Invokes philosophical understanding of the supreme
Traditionally recited for removal of ignorance born of maya
Invokes the blessing of divine grace and compassion of the lord
Purifies the mind for spiritual realization
Traditionally associated with the devotee closer to moksha
description: Among the most cherished prayers to Lord Krishna, Akrura Stuti (अक्रूर स्तुति) stands as a devotional hymn of praise. Attributed to Shrimad Bhagavata Purana 10.40, this prayer holds a special place in Hindu devotional literature. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to grant philosophical understanding of the Supreme as well as purify the mind for spiritual realization. Additionally, it is known to bestow divine grace and compassion of the Lord. Recitation is particularly auspicious on Wednesday (Budhvar) and Thursday (Guruvar) and during Janmashtami and Ekadashi. The text contains 11 verses and takes around 8 minutes to recite, fitting conveniently into a daily spiritual routine. On this page, you can read the complete Akrura Stuti in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Akrura Stuti from Bhagavata Purana 10.40 in Sanskrit with transliteration and Hindi meaning. Akrura's prayer to Lord Krishna-Vasudeva.

Akrura Stuti meaning Hindi English translation

Akrura Stuti benefits daily Vaishnava recitation

seoFaqs.question: Who was Akrura and why is his stuti significant?
seoFaqs.answer: Akrura was a paternal uncle of Krishna and a senior elder of the Vrishni-Yadava clan, renowned in the Bhagavata Purana for his deep devotion to Vishnu despite living in the court of Kamsa, the tyrant who sought to kill the infant Krishna. When Kamsa eventually summoned Krishna and Balarama from Vrindavan to Mathura, he sent Akrura as his envoy precisely because Akrura was the only courtier Krishna would trust. The journey gave Akrura the supreme blessing of being Krishna's chosen escort, and on the way he received the celebrated Vishvarupa darshan in the Yamuna river. Out of this overwhelming experience he composed the Akrura Stuti, one of the most theologically rich Bhagavata stotras. (Sources: Bhagavata Purana 10.38-40; Hindupedia Akrura; Vedabase.)

seoFaqs.question: Where in the Bhagavata Purana is the Akrura Stuti found?
seoFaqs.answer: The Akrura Stuti is preserved in the tenth canto, chapter 40, of the Srimad Bhagavata Purana, immediately following Akrura's Vishvarupa darshan in the waters of the Yamuna in chapter 39. While performing his evening sandhya bath in the river, Akrura suddenly beheld Krishna and Balarama seated on Adishesha in the cosmic ocean — the same Krishna who was riding in his chariot on the bank. Overwhelmed, Akrura emerged from the water and offered the stuti now bearing his name. The stotra spans approximately 30 verses and is one of the most extended personal-devotional praises offered to Krishna in the Bhagavata, comparable in depth to the prayers of Brahma, Bhishma and Kunti. (Sources: Bhagavata 10.40; Vedabase; Hindupedia.)

seoFaqs.question: What theological themes does the Akrura Stuti develop?
seoFaqs.answer: The stuti develops three principal themes. First, Krishna as the supreme Brahman: Akrura praises him as Param Brahman, the unmanifest source of Brahma, Vishnu, Shiva and the entire creation. Second, Krishna as the inner Self of all beings: Akrura recognises that the one Krishna seated on the chariot is identical with the Krishna seated in his own heart and in every being. Third, the dynamic of bhakti as grace: Akrura confesses his unworthiness yet recognises that Krishna's compassion has chosen him for this darshan. The stotra is therefore both a theological treatise on Vishnu's supremacy and a moving personal expression of the bhakta-Bhagavan relationship. (Sources: Bhagavata 10.40 commentaries; Vedabase; Hindupedia.)

seoFaqs.question: When is the Akrura Stuti recited and what benefits does it confer?
seoFaqs.answer: Vaishnava devotees recite the Akrura Stuti on Wednesday and Thursday (Krishna's and Vishnu's weekdays), on every Ekadashi, on Janmashtami, on Akshaya Tritiya, and during the Damodara month (Kartik). It is also recited on Akrura Tritiya, observed in some traditions as Akrura's appearance day. Benefits attributed by classical commentators include: deepening of bhakti through identification with Akrura's surrender, freedom from the ego-fear of unworthiness before God, removal of the fear of journey-related dangers (since Akrura was on a journey when he received darshan), blessing of Vishvarupa darshan in dreams or meditation, and ultimately Krishna-prema-bhakti culminating in Vaikuntha. (Sources: Hindupedia; Stotra Nidhi; Drikpanchang.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the connection between Akrura and the Syamantaka jewel story?
seoFaqs.answer: After Krishna's Mathura mission, Akrura returned to Dvaraka and in a later episode (Bhagavata 10.57) became central to the famous Syamantaka mani narrative. The Syamantaka was a divine jewel given by Surya to Satrajit, which was unjustly suspected to have been stolen by Krishna; the jewel eventually came into Akrura's possession after passing through several hands. When suspicion fell on Akrura himself, he revealed the jewel publicly, was vindicated by Krishna, and the episode established the principle that pure devotees should never withdraw from worldly affairs even when wrongly accused. Some Vaishnava acharyas trace fasting on Bhadrapada Shukla Chaturthi to this story, since seeing the moon on that night carries the curse of false accusation that Krishna himself had to atone for. (Sources: Bhagavata 10.56-57; Hindupedia; Wikipedia Syamantaka.)

seoFaqs.question: Is the Akrura Stuti suitable for daily recitation by householders?
seoFaqs.answer: Yes. Although the stotra is somewhat longer than typical daily stutis (around 30 verses, taking 12 to 18 minutes at devotional pace), it is widely recited in Gaudiya, Sri Vaishnava and Madhva traditions as a foundational Krishna-bhakti text. Householders may recite it weekly on Wednesday or Thursday, or include selected verses in daily morning Krishna puja. No initiation is required since it is preserved in the Bhagavata Purana, the most accessible Maha-Purana. The traditional preparations are simple: clean bath, white or yellow clothes, an east-facing seat, an image of Krishna or Shaligram before the devotee, tulsi mala and devotional intention. (Sources: Vedabase; Hindupedia; Stotra Nidhi.)
